titleStmt.title: Kuppalnattam copper plate, time of Tirumalai Nāyaka, year Kara

licence.p: This work is licenced under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 Unported Licence. To view a copy of the licence, visit https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/ or send a letter to Creative Commons, 444 Castro Street, Suite 900, Mountain View, California, 94041, USA.

msContents.summary: A copper plate issued in the Jovian cycle year Kaṟā (that is, Kara) during the reign of Tirumalai Nāyaka, that is, 1651 CE, as this is the only Kara year during his reign (1623-1659 CE). Records a royal order bestowing nāṭṭāmai and māṉipam to Veṅkiṭṭi Nākkaṉ. Contains a conversation in Telugu, written in Tamil script.

handDesc.p: Single-curled kompu: no distinction between medial vowels o and ō, e and ē. ḷ is used instead of ḻ, ṟ instead of r, and ṉ instead of n. Orthographic peculiarities and colloquial forms: nāṭṭāmai for nāṭṭāṉmai, māṉipam for māṉiyam, vākkāl for vāykkāl, nākkaṉ for nāyakkaṉ.

projectDesc.p: The project DHARMA has received funding from the European Research Council (ERC) under the European Union’s Horizon 2020 research and innovation programme (grant agreement no 809994).

prefixDef.p: Public URIs with the prefix bib to point to a Zotero Group Library named ERC-DHARMA whose data are open to the public.
Internal URIs using the part prefix to point to person elements in the DHARMA_IdListMembers_v01.xml file.

div.p: kaṟā Āṉi 29

matuṟā-puri-t-talat=tuk=ku
kaṟt=tar-ākiya makaṟāca-māṉiyār tiri-tirumalai-ṉā
yakkaṟ-avaṟkaḷ kāḷappaṉpaṭṭi Uḷaṭaiya
t=til vant’ iraṅki y-iruk=kum pōtu kuppalna
ttam veṅkiṭṭi ṉāk=kaṉ pātakāṇikkai
3 10 poṉṉum 4 100 Arici kāy-ka
ri ney-pāl-uṭaṉē koṇṭu vantu ṟācā
vai-k= kaṇṭāṉ

ṟācāvukku cantōcam-āki niī
ku Emi kāvelē yek=kaṭavu vit=tivi Eṉa
kēṭṭāṟ veṅkiṭṭi nāk=kaṉ coṉṉatu
nēṉu Uṇṭē kiṟāmāṉiki cātiṉappaṭṭa
yāṉiki tēvaruvāḷu Uttaṟavu kāvelē
y-eṉṟu kēṭṭāṉ

Anta-p paṭik=ku ṟācā-Utta
rāv’ āccutu
yitark=ku māl cempaṟaṇi El
laik=ki teṟk=ku
cēṭapaṭṭik= kiṟāmat=tukku kiḷḻak=ku
vī
ṟāḷampaṭṭi māluk=ku vaṭak=ku
pūcāntirak= kiṟāma
ttuk=ku mēṟk=ku
y-inta nāṅk’ elklaik=k’ uḷppa
ṭṭa kirāmam kuppalnat=tat=tuk=ku nāṭṭāmai piu
ñcai māṉipam Ūruk=ku teṟk=ku 5 caṅkili ni
lam nañcai māṉipam kirāmat=tuk=k’ uḷp
paṟavil naṭuvāyk=kālukku vaṭak=ku 4 kāṇi Āka
y-ivaḷavum nāṭṭāmai mutamai veṅkiṭṭi
ṉāk=kaṉuk=ku contam-āka Eḻuti-k kuṭut=ta tām
pura-cātiaṉa-p-paṭṭayam

y-itarkku cācci
mati
ppaṉūr cūṟappaṉāk=kaṉ
cēṭapaṭṭi perum
āḷuṉāk=kaṉ
pūcāntiṟam ciṉṉa-māya-t-tē
vaṉ

y-itai yātām orutaṟ aṭi Aḷḻit=tā
l mīṉācṭci cokkaliṅkar kēḷppāṟ

y-inta-p paṭṭayañ ceytavaṉ matuṟā-puri ⌈
mēla-vācal caṇmukam Ācāri

y-inta-p paṭi
śrī-kaṟt=taṟ-ākiya tirumalai-ṉāyak=kar-avarkaḷ tiru-k
-karat=til Oppam .
Victory to Rāma!
Year Kaṟā,That is, Kara. month of Āṉi, date 29.
At the time the glorious and honourable Tirumalai Nāyaka, alias the lord of the region of the city of Maturā,That is, Maturai. was ... Veṅkiṭṭi Nāyaka of Kuppalnattam having brought ... saw the king.
He asked ...
In that manner, there was a royal order as follows.

For this,
south of ...
east of ...
north of ...
west of ...
the village which is comprised within these four boundaries ... so that there be ... 4 kāṇis ... there is a copper-plate order that has been written and given.

For this, the witnesses are
Cūṟappaṉākkaṉ of Matippaṉūr,
Perumāḷuṉākkaṉ of Cēṭapaṭṭi,
Ciṉṉamāyattēvaṉ of Pūcāntiram.
This donation, if whoever harms it, ...
He who made this plate is Caṇmukam Ācāri at the west gate of the city of Maturā.
In that manner, the signature in the hand of the of the honourable Tirumalai Nāyaka alias the glorious lord.

The plate, rectangular in shape (height 23.3 cm, width 15.3 cm), is a typical Nāyaka period copper plate. It was found in possession of Mr. Thiruvenkatasamy in the village of Kuppalnattam/Kuppalnatham, Madurai district, Tamilnadu, who acquired it as part of his inheritance. The existence of this copper plate was unknown to scholars till now. The editor got to know of it through word of mouth.
The village names mentioned in the inscription are still in use today (phonetically intact) except one which had changed to pūcalapuram from pūcāntiram.
Edited by Ramprashanth Venkatakrishnan. Reviewed and encoded by Emmanuel Francis.
